metadata.name: Influence Process Scheduling
metadata.description: Adjust scheduling priorities for processes using nice and renice commands

hosts.name: servera
hosts.description: Target server for process scheduling exercises

tasks.name: Verify no sha1sum processes are running
tasks.description: All sha1sum processes should be terminated at the end of the exercise
hints: Terminate all sha1sum processes with: pkill sha1sum
Verify with: pgrep sha1sum or jobs command
You can also use: killall sha1sum

tasks.name: Verify nproc command is available
tasks.description: The nproc command should be available to determine CPU count
hints: nproc is typically part of coreutils package
Use nproc to see number of processors

tasks.name: Verify nice command is available
tasks.description: The nice command should be available for setting process priorities
hints: nice is part of coreutils package
Use: nice -n VALUE command to start a process with specific nice value

tasks.name: Verify renice command is available
tasks.description: The renice command should be available for changing process priorities
hints: renice is part of util-linux package
Use: sudo renice -n VALUE PID to change nice value of running process
